title: prostanthera crocodyloides
intro: Prostanthera crocodyloides, also known as the sandpaper bush or crocodile bush, is a small shrub native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, and may also have antiseptic and antifungal properties. Prostanthera crocodyloides can be used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
